mappings.rationaleAI: Literary culture connects to T'ang developments (A.1.b), Sung achievements (A.3), Ming literature (C.1.c), and Ch'ing cultural developments (C.2.b.iii).
Neo-Confucianism's resurgence addresses Sung cultural developments (A.3). Qing-era Confucianism connects to C.2.b.iii.
Mongol Empire directly addresses the conquest of China (B.1.a), Yuan dynasty (B.1.b), and cultural contacts with the West (B.2.b).
Buddhist influences connect to late T'ang (A.1.b) and Yuan religious toleration (B.2.a).
Provides context for understanding the Ming and Ch'ing dynasties' legacy and the tributary system discussed in subsection C.
Trade networks relate to Sung trade expansion (A.1.c) and Yuan commerce (B.1.b).
Dynastic succession connects to Sung (A.1), Yuan (B.1), Ming (C.1), and Ch'ing (C.2).
Global exploration connects to Ming foreign relations (C.1.b).
Economic development connects to Sung economics (A.1.c), Ming policy (C.1.b), and Ch'ing agriculture and commerce (C.2.b.i).
